title: AI 时代了，语言之争和框架之争是不是没那么重要了？
content: In the AI era, debates over programming languages and frameworks have diminished from ideological battles to practical tool choices. AI enables rapid code comprehension and migration across tech stacks, reducing switching costs. The key differentiators now are problem definition, code verification, system design, and long-term maintenance rather than language expertise. Programmers increasingly function as requirement translators, system designers, code reviewers, and complexity decomposers.

title: 有没有人有同感。。vibe coding 效率大增，但是给公司干活太亏了
content: Vibe coding dramatically boosts individual productivity by enabling one person to handle frontend, backend, and algorithms simultaneously across multiple tasks, reducing communication overhead and inducing flow states. However, workers feel exploited: increased output doesn't bring higher pay, bosses may push harder under client pressure, and upward communication remains necessary. Many now have full product-building capabilities but lack business skills to sell independently, leaving them dependent on employers despite creating complete products alone in fields like medical and legal software.

title: UniRTM，一个统一环境和工具管理软件（致敬 mise）
content: UniRTM is a unified CLI tool for managing development environments, tools, and tasks, inspired by mise. Written in Go, it uses lightweight Go shims instead of Bash wrappers for fast command routing. It manages tool versions, environment variables (with .env and SOPS encryption support), and task workflows in a single .unirtm.toml file. Features include goroutine-based concurrent downloads, mandatory version locking with checksums via unirtm.lock, and native security tool integration (Trivy, Syft). Supports macOS, Linux (glibc and musl/Alpine), and Windows natively with zero external dependencies. Licensed under MIT.

title: 月之暗面们重写估值游戏规则
content: Chinese AI startup Moonshot AI is seeking a $20 billion funding round at a $30 billion valuation—up from $4.3 billion—despite monthly active users dropping 77% from their 2024 peak. The valuation surge reflects a shift from user-count metrics to revenue fundamentals: Moonshot's annual recurring revenue hit $200 million by April 2026, driven by paid subscriptions and B-for-API usage by developers. The company repositioned from a long-text consumer app to an agent-focused model after competitor DeepSeek's 2025 breakthrough forced strategic reckoning. Industry-wide, Chinese AI firms are collectively revaluing upward as the sector proves commercial viability, though price-to-sales ratios around 150× and heavy cash burn raise sustainability questions.

title: 全球资本开始拆着买中国科技了
content:
ProShares, the largest US leveraged ETF issuer managing over $90 billion, filed to launch nine 2x single-stock leveraged ETFs tracking A-share Chinese tech companies—marking the first-ever USD-denominated leveraged products for individual A-shares. The targets include leading firms in optical modules (CIG, Eoptolink, TFC), precision manufacturing (Foxconn, Luxshare), batteries (CATL), memory chips (GigaDevice), and AI/server chips (Hygon, Cambricon).

These daily-reset leveraged products primarily serve short-term traders, not long-term investors, as volatility drag erodes returns during choppy markets while amplifying gains in strong trends. The filings signal a shift in how global capital views Chinese tech: from bundled Hong Kong/ADR plays toward targeted A-share hard technology, with potential future short products enabling two-way global price influence.

title: 通义团队再失核心？阿里首席科学家周靖人被曝离职
content: Alibaba's chief scientist Zhou Jingren reportedly resigned just six days after his June 8 appointment. The former Microsoft executive joined Alibaba in 2015 and built the Tongyi Qwen large language model team from scratch, achieving over 80,000 derivative models by October 2024. His repeated role changes in 2026—three shifts moving him from direct product control to "frontier research"—suggest a sidelining. If confirmed, he would be the fourth core Tongyi member to depart this year, following technical lead Lin Junyang and others, exposing Alibaba's struggle to balance AI research with commercialization priorities.

title: AI时代的误判：市场仍在用旧经济框架理解新经济
content:
The article argues that markets are misapplying old economic frameworks to understand the AI-driven new economy. Since 2021, a paradigm shift has occurred: US growth now stems from AI investment and wealth effects rather than monetary policy driving real estate; China's growth is shifting from property to manufacturing and productive services. AI represents a technological transformation comparable to steam engines or PCs, with infrastructure buildup lasting until roughly 2030.

The author contends AI will produce a rare "high growth + low inflation" combination through massive productivity gains and structural deflation—lower marginal costs, automation replacing labor, and "tokenization" of work. This enables a new monetary policy mix of rate cuts plus quantitative tightening (QT) to reduce debt without triggering inflation. The Federal Reserve's objectives may shift from inflation-employment to inflation-financial stability, as employment becomes less meaningful. Investors should focus on AI's earnings growth rather than broad market gains, as QT ends the era of universal

title: 突发！Anthropic全球停用Claude 5
content: Anthropic globally disabled access to its Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models on June 11 after receiving a U.S. government letter citing national security authorities and export controls. The ban applies to all foreign nationals regardless of location, including Anthropic's own foreign employees. The company shut down both models entirely rather than restricting access selectively. Fable 5 had launched just 72 hours earlier on June 9. The government provided no specific national security details, though Anthropic understood it concerned potential jailbreak methods. Other Claude models remain unaffected.

Pop Mart's World Cup partnership demonstrates efficient brand building compared to traditional sponsorships. While Wanda, Mengniu, Lenovo and Hisense spent roughly $500 million for official sponsor status with LED ads and broadcast rights, Pop Mart secured prime stadium visibility through a strategic co-branding deal estimated at $2 million upfront plus 12%-15% revenue share. The collaboration generates profit through high-margin merchandise (70%+ gross margin), acquires customers more cheaply than conventional advertising, drives sales of non-collaboration products, and builds international brand recognition valuable for global expansion, particularly in Europe and the Americas.

China's commercial space industry, after a decade of struggle since 2014 policy reforms, saw renewed investor enthusiasm in 2026, with major funding rounds and IPO pathways opening. title: 讨厌现在的工作，却又不敢辞职？试试这个办法
content: Workplace dissatisfaction needn't lead to immediate resignation. A third path exists: transform restlessness into positive motivation while remaining in your current role. First, honestly examine your core values and what truly matters to you now, separate from external success metrics. Second, give your present position a genuine chance before leaving—perseverance often builds expertise and clarity. Third, envision your ideal future work life in detail, then reverse-engineer steps toward it. Small adjustments aligned with your values can bridge the gap between present circumstances and desired fulfillment without drastic career changes.

title: iPhone 与美国生育率下降相关 | 原文
content: A study found iPhone adoption is linked to declining U.S. fertility rates. Total fertility rates dropped 22% since 2007, the year the first iPhone launched—trends unexplained by economics or childcare costs. Researchers used AT&T's exclusive 2007–2011 iPhone sales as a natural experiment, comparing network coverage areas. Results showed iPhone access reduced birth rates among 15–19-year-old women by 4.5% to 8.0%.

content: Local Chinese governments are paying millions for variety show appearances to boost tourism, with one city spending 8.5 million yuan for a single episode of "Keep Running." The purchases include filming rights, city promotion, social media content, and permanent use of the show's branding. Smaller cities lacking natural tourist appeal pursue these deals aggressively due to performance pressures and intense regional competition. Meanwhile, top-tier destinations like Anyang and Quanzhou secure free partnerships because their existing cultural prestige attracts productions without payment. The trend reflects both local governments' traffic anxiety and the variety industry pivoting to government clients as commercial advertising declines. Critics question whether these investments translate into lasting tourism returns versus ephemeral social media buzz.
